name: Portsmouth Square
note: Archive documents linked to this place; source labels honour the SFPL-vs-Democracy's-Library distinction.

documents.title: 505 Montgomery Street : final environmental impact report
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Dept. of City Planning
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1984 environmental impact report for the 505 Montgomery Street tower, analyzing the shadows it would cast on Portsmouth Square — up to about 40% of the square in some months.

documents.title: Minutes / Recreation and Park Commission
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Recreation and Park Commission
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: 1959 Recreation and Park Commission minutes on the square's use for Chinese New Year and a Guardsmen rally, and a proposed joint agreement for subterranean garage development beneath it.

documents.title: General manager's report : Open Space Acquisition and Park Renovation Fund
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Recreation and Park Department,San Francisco (Calif.). Dept. of City Planning,San Francisco (Calif.). Open Space Acquisition and Park Renovation Fund
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1975 Open Space fund report allocating $15,000 to install additional benches and shelters for the elderly at Portsmouth Square, completed October 1977.

documents.title: Theatre buildings
documents.creator: Estavan, Lawrence,Gagey, Edmond McAdoo, 1901- San Francisco stage,United States. Work Projects Administration (Calif.)
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1940 WPA history of San Francisco theatres locating the city's first schoolhouse and earliest 1849 English-language entertainment on and around Portsmouth Plaza.
